cards.back: A software testing method where the tester has no knowledge of the internal workings of the software, and tests it based on the inputs and expected outputs.
A software testing method where the tester has knowledge of the internal workings of the software, and tests it by analyzing the code and its structures.
An iterative and incremental software development methodology that emphasizes flexibility, collaboration, and continuous improvement, with a focus on delivering working software in short cycles.
A black box testing technique where the input values are divided into partitions based on their functional behavior, and then each partition is tested to ensure that the software behaves correctly.
A software development process where the test cases are written before the actual code is written, and then the code is developed to meet the test cases.
